entries.why_it_matters: Latest research showing transformer architectures illuminate neurobiology; evolution shaped brains using principles now embedded in AI.
entries.author: Various
entries.summary: Biological attention and memory mechanisms studied through lens of transformer architectures—bidirectional comparison improving both neuroscience and AI.

entries.title: The Evolution of Large Language Models: From GPT-3 to GPT-4
entries.hook: Traces LLM evolution from 175B (GPT-3) to 100T+ reasoning steps—documents how capability explosions follow scaling laws across model generations.
entries.why_it_matters: Meta-parallel: LLMs themselves evolved in silicon; each generation inherited improvements from predecessors, shows evolutionary pressure producing rapid capability growth.
entries.author: Various
entries.summary: Traces LLM evolution from 175B (GPT-3) to 100T+ reasoning steps—documents how capability explosions follow scaling laws across model generations.

entries.title: VGG: Very Deep Convolutional Networks for Large-Scale Image Recognition
entries.hook: Showed depth enables better feature hierarchies—biological visual cortex similarly has hierarchical layers from retina through temporal cortex.
entries.why_it_matters: VGG demonstrated evolutionary principle: hierarchical organization emerges as strategy for handling complexity; both vision and AI learned depth through iteration.
entries.author: Simonyan, Karen and Zisserman, Andrew
questions: How do I actually use AI well?
entries.summary: Showed depth enables better feature hierarchies—biological visual cortex similarly has hierarchical layers from retina through temporal cortex.

entries.title: GoogLeNet and Inception Modules: Multi-Scale Feature Learning
entries.hook: Parallel processing at multiple scales within single network—evolution shaped visual systems to process multiple scales simultaneously (fovea, periphery).
entries.why_it_matters: Multi-scale processing is evolutionary solution to processing variable-scale information; Inception modules recreate this principle in deep networks.
entries.author: Szegedy, Christian et al.
questions: How do I actually use AI well?
entries.summary: Parallel processing at multiple scales within single network—evolution shaped visual systems to process multiple scales simultaneously (fovea, periphery).

entries.title: The McCulloch-Pitts Neuron: Foundation of Artificial Neural Networks
entries.authors: McCulloch, Warren S. and Pitts, Walter H.
entries.hook: The original mathematical model of artificial neurons in 1943—showed biological neurons could be abstracted as logical gates.
entries.why_it_matters: Historical origin point: AI's founders explicitly modeled artificial systems on biological neurons, showing intent to recreate evolution's solutions.
entries.author: McCulloch, Warren S. and Pitts, Walter H.
questions: How do I actually use AI well?
entries.summary: The original mathematical model of artificial neurons in 1943—showed biological neurons could be abstracted as logical gates.

entries.title: The Perceptron: Origins of Machine Learning
entries.hook: First learning machine with adjustable weights—proved artificial systems could learn from error signals, key evolutionary principle.
entries.why_it_matters: Perceptron established that artificial systems could learn like evolved organisms; error-correction learning is evolution's core mechanism.
entries.author: Rosenblatt, Frank
questions: How do I actually use AI well?
entries.summary: First learning machine with adjustable weights—proved artificial systems could learn from error signals, key evolutionary principle.

entries.title: Why In-Context Learning Models are Good Few-Shot Learners
entries.hook: Theoretical explanation of why transformers rapidly adapt to new tasks from examples—implies learning-to-learn is emergent property of scale.
entries.why_it_matters: Shows rapid adaptation emerges from training; evolution similarly shaped flexible learning mechanisms; ICL proves scaling enables behavioral flexibility.
entries.author: Various
entries.summary: Theoretical explanation of why transformers rapidly adapt to new tasks from examples—implies learning-to-learn is emergent property of scale.

entries.title: Foundation Models and the Shift to Self-Supervised Learning
entries.hook: Large models pretrained on diverse data enable rapid adaptation—mirrors how evolution creates general-purpose organisms adaptable to diverse niches.
entries.why_it_matters: Foundation models show evolution's principle of generalization: develop flexible capabilities broadly applicable, then specialize for specific domains.
entries.author: Bommasani, Rishi et al.
questions: How do I actually use AI well?
entries.summary: Large models pretrained on diverse data enable rapid adaptation—mirrors how evolution creates general-purpose organisms adaptable to diverse niches.

entries.title: Explaining Biological Plausibility in Artificial Neural Networks
entries.hook: Comparing biological and artificial neural networks for learning mechanisms—identifying where they align and diverge in optimization principles.
entries.why_it_matters: Distinguishing evolved brains from trained networks helps understand evolution's specific solutions versus universal learning principles.
entries.author: Various
entries.summary: Comparing biological and artificial neural networks for learning mechanisms—identifying where they align and diverge in optimization principles.

entries.title: Synthetic Biological Neural Networks: From Implementation to Future Perspectives
entries.hook: Building neural networks from biological materials—literal hardware implementation of evolved principles in biological substrates.
entries.why_it_matters: Demonstrates evolution's principles are substrate-independent; biological neural networks in silicon and in wetware follow same computing principles.
entries.author: Various
entries.summary: Building neural networks from biological materials—literal hardware implementation of evolved principles in biological substrates.

entries.title: From AlphaGo to AlphaZero: Self-Play Evolutionary Learning
entries.hook: AlphaZero evolved superhuman play through self-play without human data—pure evolutionary competition between copies optimizing shared objective.
entries.why_it_matters: Ultimate demonstration: given only rules, AI evolved complex strategies matching human cultural knowledge, showing evolution's algorithm works in any domain with clear objectives.
entries.author: Silver, David et al.
entries.summary: AlphaZero evolved superhuman play through self-play without human data—pure evolutionary competition between copies optimizing shared objective.

entries.title: The Bitter Lesson: Compute as a Scaling Law
entries.hook: Simple algorithms with massive compute outperform complex hand-crafted methods—evolution outcompetes intelligent design through sheer scale.
entries.why_it_matters: Core principle: more compute scales better than cleverness; evolution succeeded for 4 billion years without intelligence through consistent pressure and variation.
entries.author: Sutton, Richard S.
entries.summary: Simple algorithms with massive compute outperform complex hand-crafted methods—evolution outcompetes intelligent design through sheer scale.

entries.title: AI Index Report 2025: Tracking Capabilities and Progress
entries.hook: Annual comprehensive report tracking AI capability growth across benchmarks—documents exponential trajectory of improvement across domains.
entries.why_it_matters: Empirical evidence of accelerating evolution: AI capabilities tracking exponential curves across language, vision, reasoning; documents silicon evolution's timescale.
entries.author: Various
entries.summary: Annual comprehensive report tracking AI capability growth across benchmarks—documents exponential trajectory of improvement across domains.
